Book a Discovery Call My Approach
Why Current Collective?

Current Collective didn't begin with coaching.
It began with curiosity.

I've always been fascinated by why people think, feel, and relate the way they do. Before I ever designed websites or brands, psychology was what captured my attention. At the time, though, it felt like a long and uncertain road, so I followed my creativity into design.

What surprised me was that I found myself asking the same questions.
Not about interfaces, but about the people behind them.

Working with my own therapist helped me realise there was another way to combine those interests. One built on curiosity, careful listening, and meaningful conversation. That led me into coaching, and eventually to training as a psychotherapist.

Current Collective is where those worlds meet.

My Approach
I don't have
a formula.
I have curiosity.

Maybe you've been asking yourself the same questions for a while.

Why do I keep ending up here?
Why does this relationship feel familiar?
Why do I feel stuck even when life looks okay?

Curiosity is where those questions begin to turn into understanding.

I believe change is less about breakthroughs and more about paying closer attention. I sit with people in the complexity, without rushing to resolve it.

I combine thoughtful coaching with evidence-informed practice, creating a space where curiosity leads the conversation.

I don't believe people need fixing.
I believe they deserve space to be understood.

01

Slow, intentional work

Good conversations take time. I don't rush to solutions, and I don't offer quick fixes. I create conditions for genuine reflection.

02

Human over clinical

This isn't about diagnoses or quick fixes. It's a space designed for thoughtful conversation and genuine reflection.

03

Curious, not prescriptive

I'm not interested in telling you who to be. I'm interested in exploring, together, what's already there and what might be ready to change.

Work Together

Navigate
change with
greater clarity.

I offer one-to-one coaching for people navigating change, relationships and life's bigger questions. Whether you're feeling stuck, facing a transition or simply becoming more curious about yourself, there's space here for that.

01 Individual

Knowing Yourself

Sometimes it's difficult to explain what's wrong. You just know something isn't sitting quite right.

02 Relationships

Understanding Relationships

Relationships often show us the parts of ourselves we haven't yet understood.

03 Transitions

Navigating Change

Some endings are obvious. Others happen quietly. Both deserve attention.

About

Meet Koda.
Designer.
NLP Certified Coach.
Training in Psychotherapy.

I've always been fascinated by people.

Long before I began training as a therapist, I spent over ten years teaching children and adults from different cultures and backgrounds. Every classroom reminded me that no two people learn, think or experience the world in quite the same way.

That curiosity led me into design. While designing products and brands, I found myself asking many of the same questions, not just how something should work, but how people think, decide and connect.

Wanting to understand people more deeply, I trained as an NLP coach. Working with my own therapist later showed me another path. One built on deeper reflection, evidence-informed practice and genuine human connection.

Today, Current Collective brings together design, coaching and psychotherapy training to create a thoughtful space where people can better understand themselves and navigate change with greater clarity.

Current Collective is growing alongside its founder, from coaching today toward a fully integrated psychotherapy practice. That journey isn't hidden. It's the point.
Let's Talk →

"Change doesn't always look like a breakthrough. Sometimes it looks like a quiet afternoon where something finally settles."

Perception

Some people feel things earlier

I've started to notice that I often feel things before they make sense. Before there's language for them. Before there's evidence. Before anyone else seems to register that something has shifted.

Awareness

I don't always know what I'm feeling, I just know when something shifts

There are moments when I can't name what I'm feeling. No clear emotion attached. No obvious thought. Just a subtle sense that something has moved.

Get in Touch

You don't have
to know where
this is going yet.

A discovery call is a 20-minute conversation, no pressure, no agenda. It's simply a chance to see whether we might be a good fit.

You don't need to have everything figured out before you reach out. Most people don't. That's usually the point.

What happens next
1
You send a message.
2
I'll reply within two working days.
3
If it feels like a good fit, we'll arrange a free 20-minute conversation.

Let's start with a message.

Most people don't know exactly what to say. That's okay. Just say whatever feels true right now.

Everything you share here is confidential.
